claimText: India's updated 2022 NDC targets a 45% reduction in the emissions intensity of its GDP from 2005 levels by 2030.
evidenceExcerpt: India's updated 2022 NDC targets a 45% reduction in emissions intensity of GDP from 2005 levels by 2030
examUse: Statement-based question on India's climate commitments.

claimText: India aims to achieve 50% cumulative electric power installed capacity from non-fossil fuel-based energy resources by 2030 as part of its updated NDCs.
evidenceExcerpt: India targets 50% cumulative electric power installed capacity from non-fossil fuel-based energy by 2030
examUse: Statement-based question on renewable energy targets.

claimText: The 'LiFE' (Lifestyle for Environment) mass movement is formally included as a component of India's updated Nationally Determined Contributions.
evidenceExcerpt: India's updated NDC includes the 'LiFE' (Lifestyle for Environment) mass movement component as a key to achieving its quantitative targets.
examUse: Statement-based question identifying non-quantitative components of India's NDCs.
distractorHooks: LiFE is only a domestic scheme not in NDCs

claimText: Under the Panchamrit strategy announced at COP26 Glasgow, India committed to achieving net-zero emissions by the year 2070.
evidenceExcerpt: Panchamrit commitments at COP26 Glasgow include... achieving net-zero emissions by 2070.
examUse: Correct-pair or statement-based question on India's net-zero timeline.

claimText: India's Panchamrit commitments include a target to reach 500 GW of non-fossil energy capacity by 2030.
evidenceExcerpt: Panchamrit commitments at COP26 Glasgow include reaching 500 GW non-fossil energy capacity by 2030
examUse: Statement-based question on specific Panchamrit targets.

claimText: India's NDCs include the creation of an additional carbon sink of 2.5 to 3 billion tonnes.
evidenceExcerpt: creating an additional carbon sink of 2.5 to 3 billion tonnes.
examUse: Statement-based question on forestry and carbon sink targets.
